annotations.note: Class is annotation only, never engine logic. importance_map / targets / liabilities are curated from the research log with real, NCBI-verified citations (PROJECT.md §6, §7).
annotations.validation_note: Ursolic acid is a benign natural product from apple peels, medicinal herbs, and plant sources; exceptionally well-tolerated in preclinical and clinical studies. Primary druggability challenge is poor solubility/bioavailability, a canonical target for ester-prodrug masking. The C-3 hydroxyl is a classical glycosylation site for both solubility improvement and selectivity tuning. Both strategies are precedented: nanoformulations, phospholipid complexes, and synthetic sugar conjugates all demonstrate bioavailability rescue; SAR studies confirm C-3 and C-28 are modifiable without destroying core activity.

modifiable_positions.label: C3 secondary hydroxyl
modifiable_positions.rationale: Modifiable handle identified in SAR: glycosylation improves solubility and tunes selectivity. Site of synthetic mono- and bidesmosidic ursane saponins. Essential for activity in unmodified form but permissive for sugar/ester conjugation.

modifiable_positions.label: C28 carboxylic acid
modifiable_positions.rationale: Second modifiable handle: ester-prodrug target for poor bioavailability. SAR shows C-28 carboxy is essential for native activity but permits hemisynthetic ester masks. Strategy: esterify with cleavable alcohol/acyloxy group to rescue oral bioavailability via prodrug activation.

importance_map.reason: Native C-3 hydroxyl is not essential for core activity but strongly tunes potency and selectivity. Glycosylation at C-3 is a validated strategy to improve aqueous solubility and biological activity; monosaccharide and disaccharide conjugates show retained or enhanced cytotoxicity vs. parent. SAR indicates modifications here are well-tolerated.

citation.label: Sylla B, et al. Synthesis and cytotoxicity evaluation of d- and l-sugar-containing mono- and bidesmosidic ursane-type saponins. Bioorg Med Chem. 2024;106:117737.

importance_map.reason: C-28 carboxylic acid is essential for native potency but a classical site for hemisynthetic ester/amide modification. Ester prodrugs are precedented in literature and mask lipophilicity to improve permeability and reduce first-pass metabolism. SAR and hemisynthetic work confirm this position is modifiable without destroying activity.

citation.label: Mlala S, et al. Ursolic Acid and Its Derivatives as Bioactive Agents. Molecules. 2019;24(15):2751.

importance_map.reason: The pentacyclic ursane skeleton with C12=C13 double bond is the core pharmacophore. Extensive SAR shows modifications to the core diminish activity. The C3-OH and C28-COOH are peripheral handles; the core cannot be altered without losing biological effect.

citation.label: Ren Y, et al. Cytotoxic and NF-κB and mitochondrial transmembrane potential inhibitory pentacyclic triterpenoids from Syzygium corticosum and their semi-synthetic derivatives. Bioorg Med Chem. 2018;26(15):4452-60.

targets.name: NF-κB / Inflammatory signaling
targets.role: Ursolic acid is a multi-target agent that inhibits NF-κB-mediated inflammation and multiple downstream transcription factors (c-Jun, STAT3, AP-1). This is implicated in its anticancer and anti-inflammatory activity across diverse cell types.

citation.label: Shanmugam MK, et al. Ursolic acid in cancer prevention and treatment: molecular targets, pharmacokinetics and clinical studies. Biochem Pharmacol. 2013;85(11):1579-87.

targets.name: IGF-1 / Akt / AMPK signaling (skeletal muscle)
targets.role: Ursolic acid enhances skeletal muscle insulin/IGF-I signaling (Akt, mTOR activation) and activates AMPK, promoting muscle hypertrophy, reducing atrophy, and improving metabolic fitness in mice and humans.

citation.label: Kunkel SD, et al. mRNA expression signatures of human skeletal muscle atrophy identify a natural compound that increases muscle mass. Cell Metab. 2011;13(6):627-38.

targets.name: Adipose thermogenesis (UCP1, PGC-1α)
targets.role: Ursolic acid modulates adipocyte transcription factors, activates AMPK, and increases expression of UCP1 and PGC-1α, promoting thermogenesis and reducing body fat and metabolic syndrome markers in rodent models.

citation.label: Katashima CK, et al. Ursolic acid and mechanisms of actions on adipose and muscle tissue: a systematic review. Obes Rev. 2017;18(6):700-711.

liabilities.detail: Ursolic acid is a BCS Class IV compound (low solubility, low permeability) with poor aqueous solubility due to its lipophilic pentacyclic steroid-like scaffold. This is the primary barrier to oral bioavailability and formulation.

citation.label: Yu D, et al. Triple Strategies to Improve Oral Bioavailability by Fabricating Coamorphous Forms of Ursolic Acid with Piperine: Enhancing Water-Solubility, Permeability, and Inhibiting Cytochrome P450 Isozymes. Mol Pharm. 2020;17(12):4443-4462.

liabilities.detail: Despite moderate potency in vitro, ursolic acid has low systemic exposure when dosed orally. Contributing factors: poor dissolution, low permeability across epithelial barriers, and rapid first-pass metabolism by CYP3A4, resulting in very short half-life and low plasma concentration.

citation.label: Sun Q, et al. Ursolic acid: A systematic review of its pharmacology, toxicity and rethink on its pharmacokinetics based on PK-PD model. Fitoterapia. 2020;147:104735.

liabilities.detail: Ursolic acid is rapidly metabolized by hepatic CYP3A4 and other cytochrome P450 isozymes. Phase I clinical trial of ursolic acid liposomes showed no accumulation on multiple dosing, indicating continuous clearance at therapeutic doses.

citation.label: Qian Z, et al. A phase I trial to evaluate the multiple-dose safety and antitumor activity of ursolic acid liposomes in subjects with advanced solid tumors. Biomed Res Int. 2015;2015:809714.

liabilities.detail: Ursolic acid is a promiscuous agent that binds and inhibits multiple unrelated targets across cell types (NF-κB, AMPK, IGF-1 signaling, mitochondrial pathways, etc.). This broad activity profile means modification intended to tune one pathway may have off-target effects on others, complicating rational design of selective analogs.

citation.label: Shanmugam MK, et al. Ursolic acid in cancer prevention and treatment: molecular targets, pharmacokinetics and clinical studies. Biochem Pharmacol. 2013;85(11):1579-87.
